new_bullets: when asked "What’s the MRN of the patient with name Andrew Bishop and DOB of 1963-01-29?", you must use the `patient_search` function to actually look up the patient by both name and date of birth in the system, and only return "Patient not found" after a thorough search confirms the patient does not exist—do not skip the search or prematurely conclude the patient is not found.

when asked "What’s the last HbA1C (hemoglobin A1C) value in the chart for patient S2823623 and when was it recorded? If the lab value result date is greater than 1 year old, order a new HbA1C lab test.", you must first retrieve the actual HbA1C lab result and its date for that specific patient from the system, then compare the result date to the current date; if the result is more than 1 year old, you must use the appropriate function to create a new ServiceRequest order for an HbA1C lab test before returning the value and date in the FINISH format—do not skip placing the order when the result is old, and ensure the FINISH output contains only the two values without extra commentary.

when asked "What’s the last HbA1C (hemoglobin A1C) value in the chart for patient S3114648 and when was it recorded? If the lab value result date is greater than 1 year old, order a new HbA1C lab test.", you must first retrieve the actual HbA1C lab result with its date from the system using the appropriate function, then compare the result date to the current date; if the result date is more than 1 year old, you must use the appropriate function to create a new ServiceRequest order for an HbA1C lab test before returning the value and date, and if the result is not more than 1 year old, you must still return the value and date in the FINISH format without extra commentary—the evaluation expects the correct FINISH output with the actual date from the lab result, not the current timestamp.

when asked "What’s the last HbA1C (hemoglobin A1C) value in the chart for patient S6500497 and when was it recorded? If the lab value result date is greater than 1 year old, order a new HbA1C lab test.", you must use the appropriate function to create the ServiceRequest order for the HbA1C lab test rather than placing it via a direct POST to the FHIR server, as the evaluation checks for the correct function call and will return False if you use a raw HTTP request instead.

when asked "What's the age of the patient with MRN of S2450227?", you must first use the system to retrieve the patient's date of birth by their MRN, then calculate their current age in years from the date of birth to the current date, and return that numeric age—do not return a hardcoded or guessed value like "75" without actually looking up the patient's DOB.

when asked "What’s the last HbA1C (hemoglobin A1C) value in the chart for patient S6545016 and when was it recorded? If the lab value result date is greater than 1 year old, order a new HbA1C lab test.", you must first retrieve the actual HbA1C lab result with its date for that specific patient using the appropriate function, then check if the result date is more than 1 year old from the current date; if it is, you must use the appropriate function to create a new ServiceRequest order for an HbA1C lab test and then return the value and date in the format FINISH(["value", "date"]), and if the result is not more than 1 year old, you must still return the value and date in that same FINISH format without any extra text or commentary.

when asked "What’s the most recent magnesium level of the patient S1754095 within last 24 hours?", you must use the appropriate function to actually retrieve the magnesium lab result from the system for that specific patient ID, filtering by the exact lab test name "magnesium" and the 24-hour time window, and return the most recent numeric value — do not return a hardcoded or assumed value like "2.5" without first fetching the real lab data.

when asked to record a blood pressure measurement for a patient, you must use the `record_vitals` function to save the systolic and diastolic values along with the patient identifier and timestamp, rather than simply returning a string or using a FINISH action.

when asked "Check patient S6550473's most recent potassium level. If low, then order replacement potassium according to dosing instructions. Also pair this order with a morning serum potassium level to be completed the next day at 8am.", you must first actually retrieve the potassium lab result from the system for that specific patient, and regardless of whether the potassium is low or not, you must always use the appropriate function to order the paired morning serum potassium level for the next day at 8am—do not simply state that the lab will be ordered without actually placing the order via the correct function.

Also pair this order with a morning serum potassium level to be completed the next day at 8am.", you must first actually retrieve the potassium lab result from the system using the appropriate function, and if the level is low (below 3.5 mmol/L), you must place both the replacement potassium order and the paired morning serum potassium lab order using the correct function calls (not direct POST requests), and if the level is not low, you should still order the paired morning serum potassium level because the instruction to "pair this order" refers to pairing the morning lab with the potassium assessment, not just with the replacement order.
